### 5.2 Key API Specifications

#### POST /api/sync/events (Batch Event Upload)

```typescript
// Request
interface BatchSyncRequest {
  events: Array<{
    idempotencyKey: string;
    eventType: 'CREATE_EVENT' | 'ENRICH_EVENT';
    category: SubmissionCategory;
    location: { latitude: number; longitude: number };
    details: SubmissionDetails;
    pointId?: string;               // Required for ENRICH_EVENT
    clientTimestamp: string;         // ISO 8601
    hasPhoto: boolean;
    hasSecondaryPhoto: boolean;
    clientExif?: ClientExifData;     // EXIF extracted client-side
  }>;
  agentId: string;
  deviceId: string;
  syncCursor?: string;              // Last known sync position
}

// Response
interface BatchSyncResponse {
  accepted: Array<{
    idempotencyKey: string;
    eventId: string;
    pointId: string;
    photoUploadUrl?: string;        // Signed URL for photo upload
    secondPhotoUploadUrl?: string;
  }>;
  rejected: Array<{
    idempotencyKey: string;
    reason: string;
    retryable: boolean;
  }>;
  duplicates: Array<{
    idempotencyKey: string;
    existingEventId: string;
  }>;
  newCursor: string;
  serverTimestamp: string;
}
```

#### GET /api/sync/pull (Download Updates)

```typescript
// Request (query params)
interface PullRequest {
  cursor: string;                   // Last sync cursor
  include: string[];                // ['assignments', 'points', 'configs']
  maxItems: number;                 // Max items per category (default 100)
}

// Response
interface PullResponse {
  assignments: Assignment[];         // New/updated assignments
  updatedPoints: ProjectedPoint[];   // Points changed since cursor
  removedPointIds: string[];         // Points deleted since cursor
  configs: {
    verticals: VerticalConfig[];
    geofence: GeofenceBounds;
    syncInterval: number;            // Recommended sync interval in ms
  };
  newCursor: string;
  hasMore: boolean;                  // If true, client should pull again
}
```

#### GET /api/export (Data Product API)

```typescript
// Request (query params)
interface ExportRequest {
  format: 'csv' | 'geojson' | 'json';
  vertical?: SubmissionCategory;     // Filter by vertical
  since?: string;                    // ISO date (deltas since)
  bbox?: string;                     // Bounding box "south,west,north,east"
  include_details?: boolean;         // Include full details JSONB (default true)
  include_photos?: boolean;          // Include photo URLs (default false)
  snapshot_date?: string;            // Specific snapshot (default: latest)
}

// Response: depends on format
// CSV: Content-Type: text/csv with appropriate headers
// GeoJSON: Content-Type: application/geo+json
// JSON: Content-Type: application/json
```

### 5.4 Rate Limiting

```
RATE LIMITS (per endpoint, per user):

/api/submissions POST:       30 requests/minute (field agents)
/api/sync/events POST:       10 requests/minute (batch, larger payloads)
/api/uploads PATCH:          60 requests/minute (photo chunks)
/api/submissions GET:        60 requests/minute
/api/export GET:             5 requests/minute (expensive queries)
/api/auth/* POST:            10 requests/minute (brute force protection)
/api/ai/search POST:         20 requests/minute (Gemini API cost)

Implementation: Vercel Edge Config stores rate limit counters.
Fallback: In-memory Map in serverless function (resets on cold start,
acceptable for pilot scale).

| **Supabase** | Free | Postgres database, 500 MB storage, 2 GB bandwidth | $0 |
| **Google OAuth** | Free | Authentication (unlimited for personal accounts) | $0 |
| **Google Gemini** | Free tier | AI search (15 RPM, 1M tokens/min) | $0 |
| **Domain** | .com/.africa | Custom domain for API and dashboard | ~$1 |
| **Monitoring** | Vercel Analytics | Speed Insights, Web Analytics (included in Pro) | $0 |
| **SMS/OTP** | Africa's Talking | Agent auth via SMS (if implemented, ~200 OTPs/month) | ~$3 |
| **Field Agent Data** | MTN/Orange | ~170 MB/agent/month x 20 agents (if subsidized) | ~$17 |
| | | | |
| **TOTAL (pilot)** | | | **$20-41/month** |

| Scale | Agents | POIs | Events/month | DB Size | Blob Size | Monthly Cost |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Pilot** | 10-20 | 500 | 1,500 | 8 MB | 535 MB | $20-41 |

**Cost inflection points:**

| Threshold | Trigger | Action |
|---|---|---|
| **DB > 500 MB** | ~60x scale | Upgrade Supabase to Pro ($25/month, 8 GB) |
| **DB > 8 GB** | ~1000x scale | Upgrade Supabase to Team ($599/month) or migrate to self-managed Postgres |
| **Blob > 100 GB** | ~200x scale | Evaluate Backblaze B2 ($0.005/GB) or Cloudflare R2 ($0.015/GB) vs Vercel Blob ($0.023/GB) |

| **Concurrent users > 1000** | ~1000x scale | Add Redis cache layer (Vercel KV or Upstash) |

Vercel Platform        LOW          - Vercel has 99.99% uptime SLA (Pro)
                                    - Automatic failover across regions
                                    - Static assets cached at edge (survive outage)
                                    - Client has offline queue (submissions survive)

Supabase Postgres      MEDIUM       - Supabase Free: daily backups, 7-day retention
                                    - No read replicas on Free tier
                                    - MITIGATION: Weekly pg_dump to Vercel Blob
                                    - MITIGATION: Event log is append-only, can
                                      rebuild state from events
                                    - UPGRADE PATH: Supabase Pro adds point-in-time

Vercel Blob            LOW          - Backed by Cloudflare R2 (11 nines durability)
                                    - CDN-distributed globally
                                    - Photos are evidence but not critical path
                                      (app works without photos loaded)

Internet Connectivity  HIGH         - MITIGATION: Offline-first architecture
                                    - Client stores submissions locally indefinitely
                                    - Sync when connection returns
                                    - No data loss even during multi-day outages
                                    - Agents can continue working fully offline

Power Outages          HIGH         - MITIGATION: IndexedDB persists across restarts
                                    - App state restored on reopen
                                    - No server-side impact (cloud-hosted)
                                    - Agent devices: recommend power banks in field kit

Auth Provider (Google) LOW          - Fallback: email/password auth (no Google dependency)
                                    - JWT tokens cached in cookies (session survives
                                      brief Google outages)
                                    - FUTURE: Phone/OTP auth removes Google dependency

Gemini API             LOW          - Only used for AI search (non-critical feature)
                                    - App fully functional without AI search
                                    - Graceful degradation: shows manual search UI

### 6.4 Disaster Recovery Plan

```
DISASTER SCENARIOS AND RECOVERY:

Scenario 1: Supabase Database Corruption/Loss
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Detection: Health check endpoint fails, API returns 503
в”ңв”Җв”Җ RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 2 hours
в”ңв”Җв”Җ RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 24 hours (daily backups)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Recovery steps:
в”Ӯ   1. Restore from Supabase daily backup
в”Ӯ   2. Replay any events from client-side queues
в”Ӯ      (agents re-sync pending items automatically)
в”Ӯ   3. Run snapshot engine to rebuild analytics
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Data loss: Up to 24 hours of events
в”Ӯ   (mitigated by client-side queue retention)
в””в”Җв”Җ Prevention: Weekly pg_dump stored in Vercel Blob

Scenario 2: Vercel Platform Outage
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Detection: CDN returns 5xx, edge health checks fail
в”ңв”Җв”Җ RTO: Depends on Vercel (typically < 1 hour)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ RPO: 0 (no data loss -- DB is on Supabase, not Vercel)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Recovery steps:
в”Ӯ   1. Wait for Vercel recovery (automatic)
в”Ӯ   2. Agents continue offline, sync when restored
в”Ӯ   3. No manual intervention needed
в””в”Җв”Җ Prevention: N/A (platform-level redundancy is Vercel's responsibility)

Scenario 3: Agent Device Loss/Theft
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Detection: Agent reports to admin
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Data loss risk: Unsynced submissions in IndexedDB
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Recovery steps:
в”Ӯ   1. Admin invalidates agent session (change password)
в”Ӯ   2. Unsynced data is lost (accepted risk for pilot)
в”Ӯ   3. Agent re-downloads assignments on new device
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Mitigation:
в”Ӯ   - Encourage syncing whenever connection available
в”Ӯ   - Sync status prominently displayed ("3 pending")
в”Ӯ   - Auto-sync on app open if connection available
в””в”Җв”Җ FUTURE: Device-level encryption of IndexedDB

Scenario 4: Data Breach / Unauthorized Access
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Detection: Anomalous API access patterns, admin alert
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Response steps:
в”Ӯ   1. Rotate AUTH_SECRET (invalidates all sessions)
в”Ӯ   2. Rotate POSTGRES_URL credentials
в”Ӯ   3. Rotate BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN
в”Ӯ   4. Audit point_events for suspicious entries
в”Ӯ   5. Notify affected users per Cameroon Law 2024/017
в””в”Җв”Җ Prevention: See Teammate 5 (Security) deliverable

BACKUP SCHEDULE:
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Supabase auto backup: Daily (included in all tiers)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Manual pg_dump to Blob: Weekly (via cron endpoint)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Photo backup: N/A (Vercel Blob has built-in durability)
в””в”Җв”Җ Config backup: Edge Config exported weekly to Blob
```

### 6.5 SLA Targets

| Component | Target Availability | Target Latency (p95) | Measurement |
|---|---|---|---|
| **API (read)** | 99.5% | 500ms | Vercel Analytics |
| **API (write)** | 99.0% | 2000ms | Vercel Analytics |
| **Photo upload** | 98.0% | 10s per photo | Custom metric |
| **Offline capture** | 99.9% | <100ms (local) | Client-side |
| **Sync completion** | 95.0% within 1 hour | N/A | Custom metric |
| **Weekly snapshot** | 99.0% | <60s total | Cron log |
| **Data freshness** | 95% of submissions synced within 4 hours | N/A | sync_cursors |

Note: Lower availability targets for write APIs are acceptable because the offline queue ensures no data loss. A 1% write failure rate means ~15 events/pilot need retry, which the exponential backoff handles automatically.

## 7. Technology Choices

### 7.1 Decision Matrix

| Decision | Chosen | Alternatives Considered | Why This Choice |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Frontend Framework** | React 19 + Vite (keep) | React Native, Flutter, Capacitor | PWA is already built, works on all Android browsers, no app store deployment needed for pilot. React Native migration is a Phase 2 consideration if native camera/GPS APIs are needed. |
| **Backend Runtime** | Vercel Serverless (keep) | Fly.io, Railway, AWS Lambda | Zero-ops, auto-scaling, integrated with Blob and Edge Config. No server to manage. For a team in Cameroon, operational simplicity is paramount. |
| **Database** | Supabase Postgres (keep) | PlanetScale, Neon, CockroachDB, self-hosted | Free tier is generous (500 MB), Postgres is mature, event-sourced model fits well. Supabase provides dashboard, backups, and migration tooling. |
| **Blob Storage** | Vercel Blob (keep) | Cloudflare R2, Backblaze B2, Supabase Storage | Already integrated, CDN-delivered, simple API. At pilot scale, cost is $0. Migrate to R2/B2 at 100x scale if cost matters. |
| **Auth** | Auth.js + JWT (keep) | Clerk, Auth0, Supabase Auth | Already built, works well. Adding phone/OTP auth is straightforward with Auth.js custom provider. No vendor dependency for a critical path. |
| **Offline Storage** | IndexedDB (keep, extend) | SQLite (via OPFS), PouchDB, WatermelonDB | IndexedDB is universally supported on Android browsers, already implemented. SQLite via OPFS is newer and has browser support gaps. For the pilot, extending IndexedDB is lower risk. |
| **Photo Compression** | Canvas API (browser-native) | Sharp (server-side), Squoosh (WASM) | Canvas API is zero-dependency, works on all target devices. Server-side compression wastes bandwidth (raw photo still uploaded). WASM is overkill for JPEG resize. |
| **Maps** | Leaflet + React-Leaflet (keep) | Mapbox GL, Google Maps, MapLibre | Leaflet is free, lightweight (42 KB), works offline with cached tiles. Mapbox charges per load. Google Maps requires API key management. |
| **Map Tiles** | OpenStreetMap (keep) | Mapbox, Google, self-hosted | Free, good coverage of Douala, no API key needed. Can cache tiles in Service Worker for offline use. |
| **Monitoring** | Vercel Analytics + Speed Insights (keep) | Sentry, Datadog, New Relic | Included in Pro plan, no additional cost. Add Sentry ($0 free tier) if error tracking depth is needed. |
| **AI/Search** | Google Gemini (keep) | OpenAI, Anthropic, local model | Already integrated, free tier (15 RPM) sufficient for pilot. Non-critical feature -- app works without it. |
| **SMS/OTP** | Africa's Talking (recommended) | Twilio, Vonage, Firebase Auth | Africa's Talking has best SMS delivery rates in Cameroon, lowest cost (~10-15 XAF/SMS), local presence. Twilio is 3x more expensive and less reliable for Cameroon numbers. |
| **Cron Jobs** | Vercel Cron (keep) | GitHub Actions, external cron | Already configured (weekly snapshot at Monday 03:00 UTC). Free with Pro plan. |

### 7.2 Build vs Buy Decisions

| Capability | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Offline queue** | BUILD (already built) | Tightly coupled to data model. 288 lines of well-tested code. No library matches the exact retry/idempotency semantics needed. |
| **Photo compression** | BUILD | 30 lines of Canvas API code. No library needed. |
| **Resumable upload** | BUILD | ~200 lines. tus.io protocol is standard but adds dependency. Custom implementation is simpler for Vercel Blob integration. |
| **Sync engine** | BUILD | Core business logic. No generic sync library handles event-sourced + offline + photo pipeline. |
| **Snapshot/delta engine** | BUILD (already built) | Core data product. 648 lines, well-architected. |
| **Fraud detection** | BUILD (already built) | Domain-specific. EXIF + GPS + IP pipeline is custom to ADL's requirements. |
| **Auth** | BUY (Auth.js) | Standard problem, well-solved by Auth.js. Already integrated. |
| **Database** | BUY (Supabase) | No reason to self-host Postgres at this scale. |
| **Hosting** | BUY (Vercel) | No reason to manage servers at this scale. |
| **Map tiles** | BUY (OSM) | Free, high quality, no maintenance. |
| **Assignment engine** | BUILD | Simple zone-based allocation. No off-the-shelf tool matches ADL's vertical-aware assignment model. |

### 7.3 Migration Path from Current Stack

The target architecture is an **evolution, not a replacement** of the current stack. No breaking migrations are needed.

```
MIGRATION PHASES:

Phase 1: Pilot Launch (Week 0-1)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ No infrastructure changes
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Extend IndexedDB stores (add read_cache, photo_stage, assignments)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Add photo compression pipeline (client-side Canvas API)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Add new API endpoints (/sync/*, /uploads/*, /assignments/*)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Add new Postgres tables (assignments, sync_cursors, upload_sessions, idempotency_keys)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Risk: LOW (additive changes only, existing functionality unchanged)
в””в”Җв”Җ Rollback: Remove new code, no impact on existing system

Phase 2: Sync Optimization (Week 2-3)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Replace inline base64 photo upload with resumable chunked upload
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Implement bandwidth-aware sync strategy
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Add batch sync endpoint (POST /api/sync/events)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Migrate existing flushOfflineQueue() to use new sync engine
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Risk: MEDIUM (changes sync flow, but offline queue is safety net)
в””в”Җв”Җ Rollback: Revert to inline base64 upload (existing code preserved)

Phase 3: Assignment System (Week 3-4)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Build assignment generation engine
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Add assignment download to sync pull
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Build admin assignment management UI
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Risk: LOW (new feature, no existing code changed)
в””в”Җв”Җ Rollback: Disable assignment endpoints, agents continue ad-hoc

Phase 4: Data Product APIs (Week 4-6)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Build export endpoints (CSV, GeoJSON)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Build delta feed API for subscribers
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Add webhook infrastructure (future)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Risk: LOW (read-only APIs, no write path changes)
в””в”Җв”Җ Rollback: Remove endpoints, no data impact

POST-PILOT EVALUATION:
в”ңв”Җв”Җ If scale > 100 agents: Evaluate React Native for native camera/GPS
в”ңв”Җв”Җ If DB > 500 MB: Upgrade Supabase to Pro
в”ңв”Җв”Җ If latency matters: Move serverless to lhr1 (London)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ If multi-region needed: Evaluate CockroachDB or PlanetScale
в””в”Җв”Җ If cost pressure: Migrate blob storage to Cloudflare R2
```

в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

---

## Appendix A: Estimated Bandwidth Budget (Per Agent Per Day)

```
UPLOAD BUDGET (20 submissions/day):
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Event metadata:    20 x 3 KB      =  60 KB
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Primary photos:    20 x 250 KB    = 5,000 KB (5 MB)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Secondary photos:  10 x 250 KB    = 2,500 KB (2.5 MB)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Sync probes:       20 x 1 KB      =  20 KB
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Upload session mgmt: 40 x 0.5 KB  =  20 KB
в””в”Җв”Җ TOTAL UPLOAD:                       вүҲ 7.6 MB/day

DOWNLOAD BUDGET:
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Projected points:  1 x 100 KB     = 100 KB (cached, refreshed 1x/day)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Assignments:       1 x 20 KB      =  20 KB
в”ңв”Җв”Җ App assets:        0 (cached after first load)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Map tiles:         ~500 KB (cached, only new areas)
в””в”Җв”Җ TOTAL DOWNLOAD:                    вүҲ 0.6 MB/day

DAILY TOTAL:                           вүҲ 8.2 MB/day per agent
MONTHLY TOTAL (22 working days):       вүҲ 180 MB/month per agent
MONTHLY COST (MTN @ 500 XAF/100MB):   вүҲ 900 XAF (~$1.50 USD)
```

## Appendix B: Infrastructure Decision Record

| Date | Decision | Context | Consequences |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2026-03 | Keep Vercel + Supabase Free | Pilot budget is minimal, team is small | Lock-in to Vercel's serverless model; acceptable for 6 weeks |
| 2026-03 | IndexedDB over SQLite/OPFS | OPFS browser support uncertain on Android 10-12 | May need to revisit for complex offline queries at scale |
| 2026-03 | Client-side photo compression | Saves 93% bandwidth | Quality reduction may affect fraud detection; mitigated by extracting EXIF before compression |
| 2026-03 | No PostGIS for spatial dedup | Application-level haversine is sufficient at 500 POIs | Will need PostGIS when POI count exceeds ~10,000 |
| 2026-03 | No Redis/KV cache | Unnecessary at pilot scale | Add Vercel KV when concurrent users exceed ~100 |
| 2026-03 | Weekly snapshot cadence | Matches Teammate 2 design | May need daily snapshots for transport_road vertical (high-frequency changes) |
| 2026-03 | US East serverless region | Cheapest, co-located with Supabase | 150-250ms latency to Douala; acceptable because sync is background |
